nativeRange.habitat: Marine rocky subtidal and intertidal zones; typically found attached to hard substrate in well-lit shallow reef environments

avoidWith: herbivorous fish likely to graze macroalgae (e.g. tangs, rabbitfish)

imageCandidates.notes: Product image from Mosaic Macros listing for Portieria sp. 'Elegant Lace'.

summary: A finely branched ornamental red macroalgae with a delicate lace-like thallus, available in red, pink, orange, or yellow hues. Generally suited for experienced reef keepers as a display specimen.
careNotes: Portieria sp. 'Elegant Lace' is a specialty red macroalgae (Rhodophyta, family Rhizophyllidaceae) sold exclusively by Mosaic Macros in the hobby; no AlgaeBarn or Top Shelf Aquatics listings were found. The closely related and best-studied species is Portieria hornemannii, which is also notable as the source of the antitumor compound halomon. In the aquarium, this species tends to do best under moderate full-spectrum lighting and moderate flow; excessive flow has been known to tear delicate red thalli. Red macroalgae in this family generally should not be placed on the sand bed, as contact with fine substrate has been reported to cause melting at the base. Attachment is typically accomplished by wedging into rockwork or loose-tying — super glue should be avoided, as it can cause tissue damage in delicate red species. Standard reef water quality is generally advised: nitrates 5–20 ppm, phosphate 0.02–0.1 ppm, with regular trace element supplementation (iron, iodine). As a Mosaic-only specialty entry, husbandry guidance is primarily extrapolated from family-level care practices and Mosaic's general macroalgae care guide; species-specific captive data is limited.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby source for this species. Product page confirms common name, scientific name (Portieria sp.), and lace-like morphology with red/pink/orange/yellow color variants.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Rhizophyllidaceae
additional.notes: Confirmed family (Rhizophyllidaceae) and order (Gigartinales) taxonomy; confirmed Portieria is one of four genera in the family.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Portieria hornemannii
additional.notes: Stub article; confirmed Rhodophyta/Florideophyceae/Gigartinales/Rhizophyllidaceae taxonomy. No habitat or range data available.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Macroalgae Care: The Basics
additional.notes: General macroalgae care guide used for water parameter ranges (temp 72–78°F / 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4) and red algae-specific husbandry caveats (no sand placement, avoid super glue).
